content.Text:
[Thu 2026-04-23 14:27 PDT] Create a new file `sentiment-analyzer.html` for the AgentBox website (getagentbox). This is a standalone interactive HTML page that lets users paste or type agent conversation logs and get real-time sentiment analysis.

## What to build

An "Agent Sentiment Analyzer" page - an interactive tool where users can:
1. Paste conversation logs (or use 3 preset sample conversations)
2. See real-time sentiment analysis with:
   - Overall sentiment score (-1 to +1) displayed as an animated gauge
   - Per-message sentiment breakdown in a scrollable timeline
   - Sentiment trend line chart drawn on a Canvas element
   - Emotion detection (joy, frustration, confusion, gratitude, urgency) with colored tags
   - "Emotional drift" detection - alerts when sentiment shifts dramatically mid-conversation
   - Communication health score (0-100) based on sentiment stability, resolution trajectory, and tone
3. Proactive insights section:
   - Auto-detected conversation turning points
   - Suggested agent response improvements for negative moments  
   - Pattern summary ("User tends to get frustrated when...")

## Technical requirements

- Single self-contained HTML file, all CSS inline in `<style>`, all JS inline in `<script>`
- NO external dependencies (no CDN links, no imports)
- Sentiment analysis via a simple keyword/rule-based approach (positive/negative word lists + intensifiers + negation handling)
- Canvas-based trend chart
- Dark/light theme support (CSS custom properties, with a toggle button)
- Responsive design (works on mobile)
- Match the site's visual style: use CSS custom properties like `--bg`, `--text`, `--accent` etc., modern card-based layout
- Include a proper `<nav>` with link back to index.html and the AgentBox logo

## Page structure

```
<nav> with 🤖 AgentBox logo linking to index.html
<header> with title "Agent Sentiment Analyzer" and subtitle
<main>
  - Input section: textarea + preset buttons + "Analyze" button
  - Results dashboard (hidden until analysis):
    - Gauge (CSS/SVG animated) showing overall sentiment
    - Health score card
    - Emotion tags cloud
    - Canvas trend chart
    - Per-message timeline with color-coded sentiment bars
    - Drift alerts
    - Proactive insights cards
</main>
<footer>
```

## Sentiment engine

Build a simple but effective rule-based sentiment analyzer:
- Positive words list (~50 words): great, thanks, helpful, perfect, awesome, love, excellent, wonderful, etc.
- Negative words list (~50 words): bad, terrible, wrong, broken, frustrating, hate, awful, useless, etc.
- Intensifiers: very, really, extremely, incredibly → multiply score by 1.5
- Negation: not, never, don't, can't → flip next word's sentiment
- Emoji sentiment: 😊👍❤️🎉 = positive, 😡😢😤👎 = negative
- Score each message -1 to +1, with compound averaging

## Preset conversations

Include 3 built-in sample conversations:
1. "Happy Customer" - starts neutral, becomes very positive (successful help)
2. "Escalating Frustration" - starts neutral, drifts negative (unresolved issue)
3. "Recovery Arc" - starts negative, agent recovers situation to positive

## Style notes
- Use a dark default theme with vibrant accent colors
- Gauge should be a semi-circle SVG with animated fill
- Sentiment colors: red (negative) → yellow (neutral) → green (positive)
- Smooth animations and transitions
- Cards with subtle shadows and rounded corners

Do NOT modify any other files. Just create sentiment-analyzer.html.
I’m checking the existing AgentBox site structure first so the new page matches its navigation, variables, and overall visual language before I create `sentiment-analyzer.html`.

I found the site is a flat set of standalone HTML pages. Next I’m reading `index.html` and one of the existing tool pages so the new page aligns with the nav markup, theme variables, and component treatment.

ToolUse.name: Run Select-String -Path styles.css -Pattern "--bg|--text|--accent|nav|logo" -Context 0,3 | Select-Object -First 60 | ForEach-Object { $_.ToString() }

🤖

AgentBox

Your personal AI agent that lives in Telegram

🧠

It remembers you

Your agent learns your preferences, context, and history. No more repeating yourself.

🔍

Web search built in

Ask anything about the real world — it searches the web and gives you a straight answer.

⏰

Reminders & scheduling

"Remind me in 30 minutes to check the oven." Done. It just works.

📷

Understands images

Send a photo and ask about it. Screenshots, documents, memes — it sees what you see.

🎤

Voice messages

Too lazy to type? Send a voice note. It'll understand and respond.

🔒

Your data, your agent

Private workspace for each user. Your conversations stay yours.

How it works

Up and running in under a minute

1
💬

Open Telegram

Tap the link to open AgentBox in Telegram. No app to download, no account to create.

2
🚀

Hit Start

Press the Start button and say hi. Your agent is ready — ask anything, send images, or set reminders.

3
✨

It gets smarter

The more you chat, the better it knows you. Your preferences, context, and history — all remembered.

Quick Start Wizard

Answer a few questions and get a personalized setup plan

What will you use AgentBox for?

How often will you chat?

Your personalized setup
